data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-22T01:22:10.140Z</current_datetime>

Migrate this .NET Framework code to .NET 8. Provide complete migration guidance with code and pitfalls.

```csharp
public class BackgroundProcessor
{
    private Thread _workerThread;
    private volatile bool _running;

    public void Start()
    {
        _running = true;
        _workerThread = new Thread(ProcessLoop);
        _workerThread.IsBackground = true;
        _workerThread.Start();
    }

    private void ProcessLoop()
    {
        while (_running)
        {
            try
            {
                var item = GetNextItem(); // may block
                ProcessItem(item);
            }
            catch (ThreadAbortException)
            {
                // Clean up partial work
                RollbackCurrentTransaction();
                Thread.ResetAbort(); // prevent rethrow so finally runs cleanly
            }
            finally
            {
                FlushBuffers();
            }
        }
    }

    public void Stop()
    {
        _running = false;
        if (_workerThread != null && _workerThread.IsAlive)
        {
            Thread.Sleep(2000); // give it time
            if (_workerThread.IsAlive)
                _workerThread.Abort(); // force stop
        }
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText: The user wants to migrate a .NET Framework background processor to .NET 8. This involves several key changes since `Thread.Abort()` and `Thread.ResetAbort()` are no longer supported in .NET 8. Let me provide a complete migration with explanation.

toolCalls.name: create

data.content:
Here's the migrated code. The critical pitfalls:

### What broke in .NET 8

| .NET Framework | .NET 8 | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `Thread.Abort()` | ❌ throws `PlatformNotSupportedException` | Removed — it was always unsafe (corrupted state) |
| `Thread.ResetAbort()` | ❌ same | No abort = nothing to reset |
| `ThreadAbortException` | Never thrown | Catch is dead code |

### Key migration decisions

1. **`CancellationTokenSource`** replaces both `volatile bool _running` *and* `Thread.Abort()`. One mechanism for cooperative shutdown.

2. **Pass `CancellationToken` into blocking calls** — this is the most important part. If `GetNextItem()` blocks on a socket/queue without a token, `Stop()` will hang. Make it cancellation-aware.

3. **`Task.Run`** instead of manual `Thread` — better thread-pool utilization, composable with `async/await`.

4. **No force-kill equivalent exists** in .NET 8. If code truly hangs, your only option is `Environment.FailFast()` or process-level intervention. Design for cooperative cancellation.

### Common pitfall

```csharp
// ❌ WRONG — blocks forever if GetNextItem ignores cancellation
_cts.Cancel();
_workerTask.Wait(); // deadlock

// ✅ RIGHT — bounded wait with timeout
await _workerTask.WaitAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
```

The file is at `BackgroundProcessor.cs` in your working directory.
